description.en: Fast-growing semi-aquatic leafy vegetable eaten as a stir-fried *mallung* or curry across Sri Lanka. Prefers continuously moist to submerged soils — perfect for low-lying waterlogged beds alongside rice-fallow areas. Multiple cuts possible from a single planting; ready for first harvest ~4–6 weeks after transplanting cuttings. Low input, high nutritional value — important household food-security crop.

scientific_name.quote: Ipomoea aquatica is known as Kankun in Sinhala and is also called Water Morning-Glory in English. The edible parts are the leaves.
